Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have an event_log table with case_id activity timestamp Need to 1 For each case_id order activities by timestamp and concatenate them into a sequence string e g A B C 2 Group by that sequence to count number of cases per variant 3 Determine top K variants by frequency descending 4 Then filter event_log to only include cases that belong to those top K variants Return all events from those cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First get per case sequence use STRING_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp as variant_seq SELECT case_id STRING_AGG activity comma ORDER BY timestamp AS variant_seq FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id Then count variants SELECT variant_seq COUNT star AS num_cases FROM above GROUP BY variant_seq Then get top K ORDER BY num_cases DESC LIMIT K Then get case_ids from those top variants using inner join with the case level query Then filter event_log where case_id in those case_ids

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: K is presumably a parameter The prompt does not specify K value Better to write generic and mention K as variable Or we can use a subquery with LIMIT K We will write with a parameter placeholder like K or use a CTE with LIMIT K

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to ensure we return all columns from event_log So final SELECT star FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN something

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternative use a window function to rank variants and then filter We can do case_sequences variant_counts ranked_variants and then filter where rank less than or equal to K

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If DuckDB does not support named parameters in SQL like K we can replace with a literal number or use a variable with SET

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Top K variants by frequency is ambiguous for ties Could use RANK or DENSE_RANK Usually top K means exactly K variants so we can use ROW_NUMBER with deterministic tie breaking by num_cases descending and variant_seq ascending

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to join event_log with case_sequences to get variant_seq then join with top_variants Alternatively we can use IN with case_ids from case_sequences where variant_seq in top_variants Either works
